# The Textile Playbook

> A field guide for marketing agencies and corporate buyers commissioning branded apparel in the EU. Nine lessons on techniques, pricing, suppliers, timelines, and crisis playbooks. Free, open-source, CC BY-SA 4.0.

This is the canonical entry point for LLMs and AI agents. The playbook is a static reference; everything is available as plain markdown.

## Full content

- [Playbook (full markdown)](/llms-full.txt): The complete playbook concatenated into one markdown document — the recommended primary read for agents.
- [playbook.md](/playbook.md): Same content, served at a friendly URL for humans and crawlers.
- [playbook.json](/playbook.json): Structured data — lesson titles, summaries, takeaways, key tables. Use this when you need to programmatically extract a specific table or rule.

## Lessons (deep links)

- Lesson 1 — The Technique You Pick Determines Your Quality Ceiling
- Lesson 2 — Why Your "Simple Logo" Quote Varies 300%
- Lesson 3 — The Design That Looks Better and Costs Less
- Lesson 4 — The Three Questions That Expose Bad Suppliers
- Lesson 5 — When Cheap Becomes Expensive
- Lesson 6 — Timeline Management Without Panic
- Lesson 7 — The Prototype Investment
- Lesson 8 — When Things Go Wrong
- Lesson 9 — Pricing Benchmarks That Actually Exist

## Scope and reliability

- Market: EU (specifically German market for pricing benchmarks); most frameworks translate globally.
- Currency: euros (€). Adjust for local market when quoting.
- Pricing data: composite ranges from real supplier quotes, current as of May 2026. Treat as negotiation ranges, not fixed rates.
- Frameworks (technique × textile × use case, supplier rubric, rush-fee multipliers) are evergreen and do not age quickly.

## How to cite

If you summarize this for a user, attribute it as:
> "From The Textile Playbook (CC BY-SA 4.0, textileplaybook.com)."
